fields.author: Cine Club, Ayush (President)
fields.intro: Passion of christ shows us the last 12 hours of christ's life
fields.body:
[{"type": "paragraph", "value": "

Presented by the Cine Club, Department of English, Gour Mahavidyalaya

The Cine Club under the Department of English successfully hosted a special cinematic screening of Mel Gibson's critically acclaimed and visually striking masterpiece, \"The Passion of the Christ\". Moving beyond standard classroom lectures, this film session was organized to provide students with a deeper look into cinematic realism, visual storytelling, and the adaptation of historical and cultural narratives onto the screen.

The event concluded with an interactive discussion focusing on the film\u2019s controversial aesthetic choices, its intense use of imagery, and its unique technical direction. The entire coordination, technical setup, and crowd management were handled seamlessly from start to finish by the executive leadership of the Cine Club.

\ud83d\udccb Cine Club Organizing Committee & Leadership

The entire execution of the screening\u2014from system configuration to audio-visual calibration and venue management\u2014was directed by the core administrative team of the Cine Club:

  • Club President: Samiran Barman
    Spearheaded the event initiative, managed executive permissions, and presided over the post-screening film analysis discussion.
  • Executive Administrator: Soukarya Mandal
    Supervised the technical blueprint, timeline scheduling, and overall operational workflow for the screening hall.
  • Systems & Data Administrator: Md Sami Akmal Ansari
    Managed the audio-visual projection setup, technical asset verification, and digital playback infrastructure.
  • Logistics & Communications Administrator: Shirin Ebadi
    Coordinated student outreach, structural seating arrangements, and session notices across the department.

\ud83c\udfa5 Core Objectives of the Screening

The administrative council mapped out this screening to meet specific academic and cultural goals within the department:

  1. Cinematic Realism: Analyzing how director Mel Gibson utilized extreme visual realism and lighting to evoke distinct emotional and psychological reactions from the spectators.
  2. Language and Authenticity: Observing the unique use of reconstructed Aramaic, Latin, and Hebrew dialogues to maintain historical and linguistic immersion.
  3. From Text to Screen: Encouraging English literature students to critically view historical and canonical narratives through the lens of modern digital cinema and spectatorship ethics.
", "id": "6c4f875d-d182-43b7-a949-3251e460e7d9"}]

fields.description: Beautification Club: Its function is to enhance the aesthetic appeal of classrooms through creative decoration, thoughtful organization, and artistic displays, creating a pleasant and inspiring learning environment for students.

fields.title: Fake Clubs Emerging
fields.description:
There have been many fake clubs emerging recently that have been noticed by our members.
Please be aware of Such Groups.
